title: caladenia caesarea subsp. caesarea
intro: Caladenia caesarea subsp. caesarea, commonly known as the yellow spider orchid, is a flowering plant species native to the temperate regions of Australia. This plant is known for its vibrant yellow and red flowers and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Caladenia caesarea subsp. caesarea include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically validated.
